description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the julrich/docker_qt:latest Docker image and define variables ARTIFACTS_DIR=artifacts, BUILD_DIR=build_${TARGET_ID}, TARGET_ID=linux64_gcc-6_qt-5.7. Define three stages: build, test, and deploy. In the build stage add a build job that runs g++ --version, qmake --version, creates ${BUILD_DIR}, runs cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release .., make qt5promise, make docs, copies the generated docs, library and headers into ${ARTIFACTS_DIR}/docs, ${ARTIFACTS_DIR}/bin and ${ARTIFACTS_DIR}/include, and stores the ${ARTIFACTS_DIR} directory as an artifact that expires in 16 days. In the test stage add a test job that creates ${BUILD_DIR}, runs cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Coverage .., builds the tests with make, executes ctest --output-on-failure, generates an HTML coverage report with gcovr into ${ARTIFACTS_DIR}/coverage/coverage.html, renames it to index.html, and stores ${ARTIFACTS_DIR} as an artifact expiring in 16 days. In the deploy stage add a pages job that runs only on the master branch, depends on build and test, copies ${ARTIFACTS_DIR}/docs and coverage into public/docs and public/coverage, and publishes the public/ directory as a GitLab Pages artifact, and also add an archive_tags job that runs only on tags, depends on build and test, echoes a message, moves the artifact subfolders into top-level bin, docs, include and coverage directories, and uploads them as an artifact named ${CI_COMMIT_TAG}_${TARGET_ID}.
